skill.name: python-patterns
skill.description: Guides Python framework choices, async decisions, type hints, project structure, testing, and error handling through practical decision principles.
skill.summary: Choose Python frameworks and patterns based on project context.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many apparent shell backticks, a hardcoded URL, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance terms. Review found these are Markdown fences, documentation text, and a local test URL example; no executable commands, network behavior, cryptographic implementation, reconnaissance behavior, or prompt injection were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Reported as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported external command locations are Markdown code fences, diagrams, or Python documentation examples. They do not instruct the agent to run shell commands and do not include command execution behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged backticks delimit Markdown blocks or Python examples. No shell command, subprocess call, or instruction to execute external commands is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Local Test URL Example
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL is the string http://test used as a base URL in an httpx AsyncClient testing example. It is not an external service or data exfiltration endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears inside a pytest example for an in-memory test client. It is documentation, not skill runtime behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts match ordinary documentation text, including the skill description, model design guidance, query advice, and an anti-patterns heading. No evidence found of cryptographic code or system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose only. They do not contain hashing algorithms, crypto APIs, host inspection commands, or system enumeration instructions.

content.value_statement: Python teams often choose frameworks and async patterns by habit instead of project needs. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code reason through practical Python architecture choices.
actual_capabilities: Compares FastAPI, Django, Flask, and worker frameworks by project context.
Guides async versus sync decisions for I/O-bound and CPU-bound Python work.
Recommends type hint and Pydantic usage for APIs, settings, and validation.
Suggests project structures for small scripts, medium APIs, and larger applications.
Outlines testing, fixtures, error handling, and background task selection principles.
limitations: Does not provide a complete application scaffold.
Does not run tools, install packages, or execute Python code.
Does not replace framework documentation for version-specific behavior.
Requires user context for framework, deployment, database, and team constraints.

use_cases.title: Plan a New API
use_cases.description: Choose between FastAPI, Django, Flask, and background task tools before implementation begins.

use_cases.title: Review Python Architecture
use_cases.description: Check whether services, routes, schemas, tests, and error handling match the project size.

use_cases.title: Improve Team Standards
use_cases.description: Create shared guidance for async usage, type hints, validation, fixtures, and common anti-patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Pick a Framework
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning a new Python project
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the python-patterns skill to help me choose between FastAPI, Django, and Flask. Ask me the key project questions first.

prompt_templates.title: Decide Async or Sync
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the python-patterns skill to decide where my Python app should use async code. Consider database calls, HTTP calls, CPU work, and team experience.

prompt_templates.title: Review Project Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the python-patterns skill to review my Python project structure. Recommend changes for routes, services, models, schemas, tests, and dependencies.

prompt_templates.title: Design Production Patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the python-patterns skill to design a Python API architecture with framework choice, async boundaries, validation, background tasks, error handling, and test strategy.

output_examples.input: I need an API for an AI service with many concurrent calls.
output_examples.output: The skill would recommend FastAPI for API-first async work, then verify database drivers, HTTP clients, validation needs, and background task requirements.

output_examples.input: My Django app has slow list views and duplicated query logic.
output_examples.output: The skill would suggest reviewing query patterns, using select-related or prefetch-related where appropriate, and moving common query behavior into managers or services.

output_examples.input: I am unsure how to test async endpoints.
output_examples.output: The skill would point toward pytest-asyncio, httpx AsyncClient, focused fixtures, and tests for critical API workflows.

best_practices: Provide project goals, team experience, deployment constraints, and database choices before asking for recommendations.
Use the skill for design decisions before generating large amounts of application code.
Ask for tradeoffs when choosing between framework, async, testing, and background task options.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a substitute for checking current framework release notes.
Do not force async code into CPU-bound work without a concurrency plan.
Do not apply a suggested project structure without considering project size.

faq.question: Does this skill generate complete Python applications?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on architecture decisions and implementation guidance, not full project generation.

faq.question: Which frameworks does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers FastAPI, Django, Flask, Celery, ARQ, RQ, Dramatiq, and related Python tooling.

faq.question: Can it help with async Python decisions?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains when async is useful, when sync code is better, and which libraries fit common async needs.

faq.question: Does it include testing guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers unit, integration, and end-to-end testing concepts, including async endpoint testing.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Review found no executable commands, network behavior, malware indicators, or prompt injection attempts.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Backend engineers, technical leads, and teams that want consistent Python architecture decisions can use it.
